attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
---
name: Remaining Work Before Launch
description: Feature gaps that must be addressed before the 2026-05-04 hard launch
type: project
originSessionId: 5575c1c2-2ae2-40d7-b4ef-59c8f88d8717
---
As of 2026-05-13, these items are not yet done:

1. **Lead-to-Contract workflow** — Not started.

2. **Bank Integration (USD)** — Not started.

3. **Seat-based pricing enforcement** — Removed from UI; backend never enforced seat limits.

4. **GitHub repo → private** — Settings-only change, no code.

**Deferred (blocked):**
- **Server-side feature gating** — Blocked until user obtains a USD Paystack account (using Crevia itself for that process). Client-side limits exist for now.

**Completed:** SMTP fixed (Resend SMTP configured in Supabase Auth), WhatsApp chat (grouping, presence, read receipts), 36 premium Crevia Link themes, contract send edge function, footer logo, Pricing page cleanup, PWA install banner. Full mobile responsiveness pass (invoices, contracts, Crevia Link sub-tabs, InvoicePreviewDialog). New Crevia logo rolled out across all pages with ring styling. SendDocumentDialog workspace DM send option added. Nav link spacing widened. "Own Your Story" CTA copy. Frontend changes paused — to be continued later.

**Why:** Lead-to-contract and bank integration are the next product features. Feature gating is deferred due to Paystack USD account dependency.

**How to apply:** When the user asks what to work on next, surface lead-to-contract first, then bank integration. Do not suggest server-side gating until Paystack USD is sorted.

9	
10	Production URL: https://creviamvp.vercel.app
11	Primary branch: main (deploys directly to Vercel)
12	
13	Stack: React + TypeScript (Vite), Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage), Vercel, Paystack (payments), Web Crypto API (E2EE).
14	
15	## Status (2026-05-16)
16	App is live and deployed. Launch-critical work is done.
17	
18	## Admin dashboard (src/pages/Admin.tsx)
19	Fully built out. Sections: Overview, Users, Billing, Documents, Templates, Support, Settings.
20	
21	## Key decisions
22	- Plans are Free / Pro / Enterprise (no "Creative Pro" / "Brand Workspace" — those portals were removed)
23	- DB stores plan as "free" / "pro" / "enterprise"
24	- Plan prices stored in app_settings (keys: plan_price_pro, plan_price_enterprise) — admin changes reflect on Pricing page and Paystack charge amount
25	- Ops layer cards: Crevia Link + Chat → /crevia-studio, Invoice → /crevia-invoice, Contracts → /crevia-contracts
26	- Auth guard on ops cards: unauthenticated users redirected to /auth?mode=signup&redirect=<path>
27	
28	## Migrations applied to prod (all through 20260516200000)
29	- support_ticket_replies (admin_reply, replied_at on support_tickets)
30	- soft_delete_documents (deleted_at on invoices and contracts)
31	- support_ticket_type (type column on support_tickets)
32

file.content:
---
name: project-context
description: "Crevia MVP — prod URL, stack, launch status, completed features"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 4ccc2eea-20db-4970-9039-2ca6cb66255a
---

Production URL: https://creviamvp.vercel.app
Primary branch: main (deploys directly to Vercel)

Stack: React + TypeScript (Vite), Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage), Vercel, Paystack (payments), Web Crypto API (E2EE).

## Status (2026-05-16)
App is live and deployed. Launch-critical work is done.

## Admin dashboard (src/pages/Admin.tsx)
Fully built out. Sections: Overview, Users, Billing, Documents, Templates, Support, Settings.

## Key decisions
- Plans are Free / Pro / Enterprise (no "Creative Pro" / "Brand Workspace" — those portals were removed)
- DB stores plan as "free" / "pro" / "enterprise"
- Plan prices stored in app_settings (keys: plan_price_pro, plan_price_enterprise) — admin changes reflect on Pricing page and Paystack charge amount
- Ops layer cards: Crevia Link + Chat → /crevia-studio, Invoice → /crevia-invoice, Contracts → /crevia-contracts
- Auth guard on ops cards: unauthenticated users redirected to /auth?mode=signup&redirect=<path>

## Migrations applied to prod (all through 20260516200000)
- support_ticket_replies (admin_reply, replied_at on support_tickets)
- soft_delete_documents (deleted_at on invoices and contracts)
- support_ticket_type (type column on support_tickets)

content.text:
Based on memory, here's where things stand:

**Last session** (git log confirms): security work — webhook audit log + rate limiting on invoice/contract creation, and dynamic pricing from admin.

**What's left before full launch:**
1. **Lead-to-contract workflow** — Not started (highest priority)
2. **Bank integration (USD)** — Not started
3. **Seat-based pricing enforcement** — Backend never enforced seat limits
4. **GitHub repo → private** — Settings-only change

**Deferred:** Server-side feature gating (blocked on Paystack USD account).

The most recent commits suggest you were hardening the security layer. The natural next thing would be the **lead-to-contract workflow** — want to pick that up, or is there something else you had in mind?

input.command:
git add supabase/functions/contract-send/index.ts supabase/functions/invoice-send/index.ts supabase/functions/feedback-notify/index.ts supabase/functions/login-alert/index.ts supabase/functions/verification-notify/index.ts src/pages/ReceivedDocuments.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(email): add Crevia logo to all email templates; fix contract button deep-link

- Replace text "Crevia" header with logo image (crevia-logo.png) across all 5
  email functions: contract-send, invoice-send, feedback-notify, login-alert,
  verification-notify
- Contract email CTA now links to /received?tab=contracts&id=<contract_id>
  instead of the generic /received page
- ReceivedDocuments auto-opens the matching contract/invoice when ?id= is present
  in the URL (supports arriving directly from email button click)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Yes, I know eTIMS well. Great topic, especially relevant to Crevia.

---

## eTIMS — Electronic Tax Invoice Management System

**What it is:** A system by the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) that requires businesses to generate and submit tax invoices digitally, in real-time, so KRA can track VAT and income tax.

### Why it matters for Crevia
Any Kenyan business using Crevia to invoice clients that is VAT-registered **must** comply. If Crevia doesn't support eTIMS, those users can't legally use it for invoicing.

### How it works (the flow)

```
Your business creates invoice
        ↓
Submit invoice data to KRA's eTIMS API
        ↓
KRA validates & returns:
  - Control Unit Invoice Number (CUIN)
  - QR code / verification code
        ↓
You print that code ON the invoice
        ↓
Client receives a KRA-verified invoice
```

### What KRA needs from each invoice
- Seller PIN (KRA PIN)
- Buyer PIN (if registered)
- Invoice date, number, items
- Tax breakdown (VAT at 16%)
- Totals

### What Crevia would need to build
1. A field for the user's **KRA PIN** in business settings
2. A call to the **eTIMS API** when an invoice is finalized/sent
3. Store and display the returned **CUIN + QR code** on the invoice PDF/email

---

Want me to go deeper on any part — the API itself, the data format KRA expects, or how you'd architect the integration in Crevia?

message.content: yes go deeper on the API and data format and i will do it for crevia(the steps)

content.text:
## eTIMS API — Deep Dive

---

### 1. Integration Type for Crevia

KRA offers three options. For a SaaS, you want **OSCU (Online Sales Control Unit)** — pure API, no physical device.

| Type | What it is | Right for |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| ESCU | Physical hardware device | Retail shops |
| VSCU | Desktop software | Standalone businesses |
| **OSCU** | **REST API** | **SaaS platforms like Crevia** |

---

### 2. Base URLs

```
Sandbox:    https://etims-api-sbx.kra.go.ke/etims-api
Production: https://etims-api.kra.go.ke/etims-api
```

---

### 3. Key Endpoints You'll Use

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `/selectInitOsdcInfo` | POST | Initialize/register your device with KRA |
| `/selectTaxpayerInfo` | POST | Validate a buyer's KRA PIN |
| `/selectCodeList` | POST | Fetch KRA reference codes (item types, units, etc.) |
| `/insertTrnsSalesOsdc` | POST | **Submit an invoice** ← main one |
| `/insertTrnsSalesCreditNote` | POST | Submit a credit note (refund) |

---

### 4. Authentication

Every request needs these headers:
```http
Content-Type: application/json
tin: P051234567X        ← seller's KRA PIN
bhfId: 00              ← branch ID (00 = head office)
cmcKey: <device_key>   ← given after initialization
```

The `cmcKey` is a secret key KRA gives you after you call `/selectInitOsdcInfo` with your PIN and device serial number. You store it per-user in Crevia's `business_settings`.

---

### 5. Invoice Submission — Data Format

This is the body for `POST /insertTrnsSalesOsdc`:

```json
{
  "tin": "P051234567X",
  "bhfId": "00",
  "orgInvcNo": null,
  "cisInvcNo": "INV-2026-001",
  "custTin": "P051111111A",
  "custNm": "Client Name",
  "rcptTyCd": "S",
  "pmtTyCd": "01",
  "salesSttsCd": "02",
  "cfmDt": "20260516153000",
  "salesDt": "20260516",
  "totItemCnt": 2,

  "taxblAmtA": 0,
  "taxblAmtB": 8620.69,
  "taxblAmtC": 0,
  "taxblAmtD": 0,
  "taxAmtA": 0,
  "taxAmtB": 1379.31,
  "taxAmtC": 0,
  "taxAmtD": 0,
  "totTaxblAmt": 8620.69,
  "totTaxAmt": 1379.31,
  "totAmt": 10000.00,

  "itemList": [
    {
      "itemSeq": 1,
      "itemNm": "Brand Design",
      "qty": 1,
      "prc": 10000,
      "splyAmt": 10000,
      "dcRt": 0,
      "dcAmt": 0,
      "taxTyCd": "B",
      "taxblAmt": 8620.69,
      "taxAmt": 1379.31,
      "totAmt": 10000
    }
  ],

  "pmtList": [
    {
      "payTyCd": "01",
      "payAmt": 10000
    }
  ]
}
```

**Key fields explained:**

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `rcptTyCd` | `S` = Sale, `R` = Refund |
| `pmtTyCd` | `01` = Cash, `02` = Credit, `03` = M-Pesa |
| `salesSttsCd` | `02` = Approved (always send this) |
| `cfmDt` | Confirmation datetime `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` |
| `taxTyCd` | **`A`** = Exempt, **`B`** = 16% VAT, **`C`** = 8%, **`D`** = Zero-rated |
| `taxblAmt` | Amount **before** tax (i.e. `total / 1.16`) |
| `taxAmt` | The VAT portion (`total - taxblAmt`) |

---

### 6. KRA's Response

```json
{
  "resultCd": "000",
  "resultMsg": "Successful",
  "data": {
    "rcptNo": 4821,
    "intrlData": "abc123xyz...",
    "rcptSign": "SHA256-signature-string",
    "sdcId": "SDC0001",
    "totalAmount": 10000
  }
}
```

| Field | What to store |
|-------|---------------|
| `rcptNo` | KRA's receipt number — goes on the invoice |
| `rcptSign` | Signature — used to generate the QR code |
| `intrlData` | Internal data string — also in the QR |

The **QR code content** is typically:
```
{tin}_{rcptNo}_{rcptSign}_{salesDt}_{totAmt}
```

---

### 7. The Steps You'd Build in Crevia

```
1. Business Settings
   └─ Add: KRA PIN, bhfId, cmcKey (after init)

2. One-time: Device Initialization
   └─ POST /selectInitOsdcInfo → store cmcKey in business_settings

3. On "Send Invoice"
   └─ Edge function: map invoice → eTIMS payload
   └─ POST /insertTrnsSalesOsdc
   └─ Store rcptNo + rcptSign on the invoice row

4. Invoice PDF/Email
   └─ Show KRA receipt number
   └─ Generate QR code from rcptSign data
   └─ Show "eTIMS Verified" badge
```

---

### 8. The VAT Math (important)

KRA works with **tax-exclusive** amounts. So if a client pays KES 10,000:

```
taxblAmt = 10000 / 1.16 = 8620.69   ← amount before VAT
taxAmt   = 10000 - 8620.69 = 1379.31 ← the VAT
totAmt   = 10000
```

This is different from how Crevia currently stores totals (tax-inclusive), so you'll need to compute these on the fly during submission.

---

Where do you want to go next — the edge function code structure, the business settings schema changes, or the QR code generation?

lastPrompt: yes go deeper on the API and data format and i will do it for crevia(the steps)

content: Teaching eTIMS integration for Crevia invoices. Covered the OSCU API endpoints, authentication, invoice payload format, VAT math, and response fields. Next: decide whether to start with the edge function code, business settings schema, or QR code generation.

message.content: how long will it take to implemnt it in crevia

content.text:
Honest estimate:

---

### Phase Breakdown

| Phase | What | Time |
|-------|------|------|
| **1. KRA Sandbox access** | Apply for OSCU developer credentials, get sandbox PIN + cmcKey | **1–3 days** (waiting on KRA) |
| **2. Business Settings** | Add KRA PIN, bhfId, cmcKey fields to DB + settings UI | **2–3 hours** |
| **3. Device Init** | Edge function to call `/selectInitOsdcInfo`, store cmcKey | **1–2 hours** |
| **4. Invoice submission** | Edge function: map Crevia invoice → eTIMS payload, call KRA, store rcptNo + rcptSign | **4–6 hours** |
| **5. Invoice display** | Show KRA receipt number + QR code on invoice PDF/email | **2–3 hours** |
| **6. Error handling** | KRA API goes down, invalid PIN, network retries | **2–3 hours** |
| **7. Testing** | End-to-end on sandbox, edge cases (exempt items, credit notes) | **1–2 days** |

---

### Realistic Total

| Scenario | Time |
|----------|------|
| KRA responds fast, no blockers | **3–4 days** |
| KRA sandbox takes time (typical) | **1–2 weeks** |

---

### The Real Bottleneck

The **code itself is maybe 1 day of work**. The slow part is:

1. **KRA sandbox approval** — you have to register as a software integrator on their eTIMS portal. They sometimes take days to respond.
2. **Their API docs** — incomplete in places, you'll hit undocumented errors that require trial and error.
3. **VAT-only requirement** — eTIMS is currently mandatory for VAT-registered businesses. If most Crevia users aren't VAT-registered, you can make it optional for now and gate it behind a toggle in settings.

---

### My Suggestion

Make it **opt-in** first — a toggle in Business Settings: *"I am VAT registered (eTIMS)"*. Only users who turn it on go through the KRA flow. That way you ship it without breaking anything for non-VAT users.

Want to start with the KRA sandbox registration steps?

message.content: give me the steps i share with my team members and how long it will take

content.text:
## Crevia × eTIMS Integration — Team Implementation Guide

---

### Prerequisites (Before Writing Any Code)
**Owner: Anyone with access to KRA eTIMS portal**
**Time: 1–3 days (KRA dependent)**

1. Go to `https://etims.kra.go.ke`
2. Register as a **Software Integrator** (OSCU type)
3. Submit your business details + KRA PIN
4. KRA will issue you:
   - Sandbox TIN (test KRA PIN)
   - Sandbox `cmcKey` (device key)
   - Access to the sandbox environment `https://etims-api-sbx.kra.go.ke`
5. Download KRA's eTIMS API documentation from the portal (they provide a PDF)

> **Don't start coding until you have sandbox credentials — you need real keys to test anything.**

---

### Step 1 — Database Migration
**Owner: Backend dev**
**Time: 1 hour**

Add these columns to `business_settings` table in Supabase:

```sql
alter table business_settings
  add column if not exists kra_pin text,
  add column if not exists etims_bhf_id text default '00',
  add column if not exists etims_cmc_key text,
  add column if not exists etims_enabled boolean default false;
```

Add these columns to the `invoices` table:

```sql
alter table invoices
  add column if not exists etims_rcpt_no integer,
  add column if not exists etims_rcpt_sign text,
  add column if not exists etims_submitted_at timestamptz;
```

---

### Step 2 — Business Settings UI
**Owner: Frontend dev**
**Time: 2–3 hours**

In `StudioSettingsTab.tsx`, add an **eTIMS section** under the existing business settings form:

- Toggle: `I am VAT registered (eTIMS)` → sets `etims_enabled`
- Input: `KRA PIN` → saves to `kra_pin`
- Input: `Branch ID` → saves to `etims_bhf_id` (default `00`)
- Button: `Initialize Device` → calls the init edge function (Step 3)
- Show a green "Device Active" badge if `etims_cmc_key` is set

---

### Step 3 — Device Initialization Edge Function
**Owner: Backend dev**
**Time: 2 hours**

Create `supabase/functions/etims-init/index.ts`

What it does:
- Takes the user's `kra_pin` and `bhf_id`
- Calls `POST https://etims-api-sbx.kra.go.ke/etims-api/selectInitOsdcInfo`
- Gets back `cmcKey`
- Saves `cmcKey` to `business_settings`

Request body to KRA:
```json
{
  "tin": "P051234567X",
  "bhfId": "00",
  "dvcSrlNo": "CREVIA-{user_id}"
}
```

Expected response:
```json
{
  "resultCd": "000",
  "data": {
    "rcptNo": 0,
    "intrlData": "...",
    "cmcKey": "abc123..."
  }
}
```

Save `cmcKey` → `business_settings.etims_cmc_key`

---

### Step 4 — Invoice Submission Edge Function
**Owner: Backend dev**
**Time: 4–6 hours**

Create `supabase/functions/etims-submit/index.ts`

This is the core function. It:
1. Receives an `invoice_id`
2. Fetches the invoice + line items + business settings from Supabase
3. Maps the data to KRA's format (the VAT math is critical here)
4. Calls `POST /insertTrnsSalesOsdc`
5. Saves `rcptNo`, `rcptSign`, `etims_submitted_at` back to the invoice

**VAT math helper to include:**
```typescript
function splitVAT(totalInclusive: number) {
  const taxblAmt = parseFloat((totalInclusive / 1.16).toFixed(2));
  const taxAmt = parseFloat((totalInclusive - taxblAmt).toFixed(2));
  return { taxblAmt, taxAmt };
}
```

**Call this function** from inside the existing `invoice-send` edge function — right before sending the email, if `etims_enabled = true`.

---

### Step 5 — Show KRA Details on Invoice
**Owner: Frontend dev**
**Time: 2–3 hours**

In `InvoicePreviewDialog.tsx` and the invoice email template:

- If `invoice.etims_rcpt_no` exists, show:
  - `KRA Receipt No: {etims_rcpt_no}`
  - A QR code generated from: `{kra_pin}_{rcptNo}_{rcptSign}_{date}_{total}`
  - Small "eTIMS Verified" badge

For QR code generation use the `qrcode` npm package on the frontend, or generate it server-side in the edge function using a Deno QR library.

---

### Step 6 — Error Handling
**Owner: Backend dev**
**Time: 2 hours**

Handle these specific cases:

| KRA Error Code | Meaning | What to do |
|----------------|---------|------------|
| `001` | System error | Retry once, then notify user |
| `004` | Invalid TIN | Show "Check your KRA PIN in settings" |
| `801` | Duplicate invoice | Already submitted, skip silently |
| Network timeout | KRA API down | Queue for retry, don't block invoice send |

**Important:** Never block an invoice from being sent just because eTIMS fails. Send the invoice first, flag it as `etims_pending`, retry submission in the background.

---

### Step 7 — Testing on Sandbox
**Owner: Both devs**
**Time: 1–2 days**

Test these scenarios:
- [ ] Standard invoice (16% VAT, single item)
- [ ] Multi-item invoice
- [ ] Invoice with discount
- [ ] Zero-rated invoice (export client)
- [ ] VAT-exempt invoice
- [ ] KRA API timeout (turn off network mid-request)
- [ ] Duplicate submission (same invoice sent twice)
- [ ] Invalid KRA PIN in settings

---

### Summary Timeline

```
Day 1–3:   KRA sandbox registration (waiting)
Day 3:     DB migration + settings UI (Step 1 & 2)
Day 4:     Init edge function (Step 3)
Day 5–6:   Invoice submission function (Step 4)
Day 6:     Invoice display + QR code (Step 5)
Day 7:     Error handling (Step 6)
Day 8–9:   Full sandbox testing (Step 7)
Day 10:    Switch to production KRA credentials, deploy
```

**Total: ~2 weeks** (most of week 1 is waiting on KRA)

---

### Who Does What

| Task | Role |
|------|------|
| KRA portal registration | Anyone / Team lead |
| Steps 1, 3, 4, 6 | Backend dev |
| Steps 2, 5 | Frontend dev |
| Step 7 | Both |

---

> **Key rule:** Make eTIMS **opt-in**. Users who aren't VAT-registered should never see this flow. Gate everything behind `etims_enabled = true` in business settings.
